summary: Prevent Deepgram cloud dictation from clipping the beginning or end of push-to-talk recordings.

review_items.summary: Cloud push-to-talk arms native capture before opening the Deepgram WebSocket, then streams the buffered capture backlog when Deepgram attaches so the 500ms pre-roll and any connection-delay audio are sent to cloud transcription.
Normal Deepgram push-to-talk release keeps the realtime audio stream open for 500ms after release before closing, reducing clipped final words without changing local Whisper capture.
No database schema, migration, SQL policy, hosted API, auth, billing, or cross-project contract change is required.

patches.summary: Arm cloud capture before opening Deepgram and wait 500ms after push-to-talk release before stopping the cloud realtime stream.
When a Deepgram realtime stream attaches after capture has already started, send the buffered captured chunks from the native ring buffer before streaming live chunks.

compatibility.notes: The change is local to the desktop app. Local Whisper transcription, recorder shortcuts, provider selection, API key handling, and transcript insertion remain available.

data_handling.sends_audio_to_remote_service: Only when the user selects cloud mode; Deepgram now receives the same 500ms pre-roll-backed capture window plus 500ms of release tail audio for normal push-to-talk completion.
data_handling.stores_transcripts: The latest transcript continues to use local WebView result storage.
data_handling.api_key_storage: Unchanged; the Deepgram API key stays in local WebView storage and is not written to policy files.

tests.summary: Validate Tauri commands and audio host code.
